quizQuestion:
{___} il fasse froid, nous sortirons ce soir.
(Although it's cold, we will go out tonight.)
sentenceTranslation: Although it's cold, we will go out tonight.
explanation: bien que introduces a concession clause and always triggers the subjonctif. Here: fasse (subjunctive of faire).

quizQuestion:
{___} il soit riche, il vit très simplement.
(Although he is rich, he lives very simply.)
sentenceTranslation: Although he is rich, he lives very simply.
explanation: quoique is a formal synonym of bien que and also requires the subjonctif: soit (subjunctive of être).

quizQuestion:
Je suis sorti {___} la pluie.
(In spite of the rain, I went out.)
sentenceTranslation: In spite of the rain, I went out.
explanation: malgré (despite / in spite of) is followed directly by a noun, not a verb. Contrast with bien que which takes the subjonctif.

quizQuestion:
{___} ses efforts, il a échoué à l'examen.
(Despite his efforts, he failed the exam.)
sentenceTranslation: Despite his efforts, he failed the exam.
grammarPointLabel: en dépit de + nom — concession formelle
explanation: en dépit de (despite) is a formal alternative to malgré, also followed by a noun phrase. It carries a slightly more emphatic register.

quizQuestion:
{___}, il travaille bien, mais il est souvent en retard.
(Granted, he works well, but he is often late.)
sentenceTranslation: Granted, he works well, but he is often late.
explanation: certes (granted / admittedly) opens a concession; mais introduces the contrasting reality. The pair certes…mais is a hallmark of formal B2 argumentation.

quizQuestion:
La quantité est insuffisante ; {___}, le produit est de bonne qualité.
(The quantity is insufficient; however, the product is good quality.)
sentenceTranslation: The quantity is insufficient; however, the product is good quality.
explanation: néanmoins (nonetheless / however) follows a semicolon or period to express a formal concession. Synonym of toutefois and cependant in formal registers.

quizQuestion:
Il a réussi l'examen ; {___}, ses résultats restent en dessous de la moyenne.
(He passed the exam; however, his results remain below average.)
sentenceTranslation: He passed the exam; however, his results remain below average.
explanation: toutefois (however / nevertheless) qualifies the preceding statement with a formal reservation. Interchangeable with néanmoins in most written contexts.

quizQuestion:
Il a {___} essayer, il ne comprend pas cette règle.
(No matter how hard he tries, he doesn't understand this rule.)
sentenceTranslation: No matter how hard he tries, he doesn't understand this rule.
grammarPointLabel: avoir beau + infinitif — concession d'effort vain
explanation: avoir beau + infinitif expresses futile effort: no matter how much one does X, the result is still Y. Unique to French; the main clause does not require a special mood.

quizQuestion:
{___} nous ayons peu de temps, nous terminerons ce projet.
(Although we have little time, we will finish this project.)
sentenceTranslation: Although we have little time, we will finish this project.
explanation: bien que requires the subjonctif: ayons (subjunctive of avoir). The main clause uses the future. bien que and quoique are interchangeable here.

quizQuestion:
{___} la fatigue, elle a continué à travailler toute la nuit.
(Despite her fatigue, she kept working all night.)
sentenceTranslation: Despite her fatigue, she kept working all night.
explanation: malgré is followed by a noun (la fatigue). To use a clause, switch to bien que + subjonctif: bien qu'elle soit fatiguée.

quizQuestion:
{___} la solution paraisse simple, sa mise en œuvre est complexe.
(Although the solution seems simple, its implementation is complex.)
sentenceTranslation: Although the solution seems simple, its implementation is complex.
explanation: quoique requires the subjonctif: paraisse (from paraître). quoique and bien que are interchangeable; quoique is the more literary choice.

quizQuestion:
Elle a {___} répéter ses explications, personne ne comprenait.
(No matter how many times she repeated her explanations, no one understood.)
sentenceTranslation: No matter how many times she repeated her explanations, no one understood.
grammarPointLabel: avoir beau + infinitif — concession d'effort répété vain
explanation: avoir beau + infinitif signals persistent action without the expected result. Structure: subject + avoir beau + infinitive; main clause states the frustrating reality.

quizQuestion:
Il travaille dur {___} son frère se repose toute la journée.
(He works hard while his brother rests all day.)
sentenceTranslation: He works hard while his brother rests all day.
explanation: tandis que contrasts two simultaneous actions to highlight opposition, not mere temporal coincidence. More literary than pendant que.

quizQuestion:
Elle a réussi {___} tout le monde pensait qu'elle allait échouer.
(She succeeded while everyone thought she would fail.)
sentenceTranslation: She succeeded while everyone thought she would fail.
explanation: alors que expresses a surprising contrast between two facts. It can also mark simultaneity, but here it emphasises the unexpectedness of the outcome.

quizQuestion:
Son français est excellent ; {___}, son anglais laisse à désirer.
(His French is excellent; on the other hand, his English leaves something to be desired.)
sentenceTranslation: His French is excellent; on the other hand, his English leaves something to be desired.
explanation: en revanche (on the other hand) is a formal written connector that places two contrasting situations side by side. Preferred over par contre in written and professional registers.

quizQuestion:
Je n'aime pas le cinéma ; {___}, j'adore le théâtre.
(I don't like cinema; on the other hand, I love theatre.)
sentenceTranslation: I don't like cinema; on the other hand, I love theatre.
explanation: par contre is the informal / spoken equivalent of en revanche. Used in everyday speech and informal writing; avoided in formal essays. Semantically identical to en revanche.

quizQuestion:
Ce n'est pas difficile ; {___}, c'est très simple une fois qu'on comprend.
(It's not difficult; on the contrary, it's very simple once you understand.)
sentenceTranslation: It's not difficult; on the contrary, it's very simple once you understand.
explanation: au contraire (on the contrary) directly negates what was just said, signalling a complete reversal of the preceding idea. Stronger than en revanche.

quizQuestion:
{___} ce que l'on pensait, le projet a été un grand succès.
(Contrary to what was expected, the project was a great success.)
sentenceTranslation: Contrary to what was expected, the project was a great success.
grammarPointLabel: contrairement à — opposition formelle + nom / clause relative
explanation: contrairement à (contrary to / unlike) is followed by a noun or relative clause (ce que). Formal register, common in reports and academic writing.

quizQuestion:
Les prix augmentent {___} les salaires restent stables.
(Prices rise while wages remain stable.)
sentenceTranslation: Prices rise while wages remain stable.
grammarPointLabel: tandis que — opposition de tendances simultanées
explanation: tandis que highlights the simultaneous contrast between rising prices and static wages. alors que could also fit, emphasising the surprising nature of the contrast.

quizQuestion:
La première approche a échoué. {___}, la méthode expérimentale a produit d'excellents résultats.
(The first approach failed. Conversely, the experimental method produced excellent results.)
sentenceTranslation: The first approach failed. Conversely, the experimental method produced excellent results.
explanation: à l'inverse (conversely / on the other hand) presents a direct reversal of the preceding situation. Formal register, common in academic and scientific writing.

quizQuestion:
Certains pays connaissent une croissance rapide {___} d'autres stagnent économiquement.
(Some countries experience rapid growth while others stagnate economically.)
sentenceTranslation: Some countries experience rapid growth while others stagnate economically.
grammarPointLabel: alors que — opposition de situations simultanées
explanation: alors que highlights the contrast between two simultaneous economic realities. tandis que could also fit, though alors que more strongly emphasises the unexpected nature of the contrast.

quizQuestion:
Le premier trimestre a été décevant ; {___}, le second trimestre affiche des résultats très encourageants.
(The first quarter was disappointing; on the other hand, the second quarter shows very encouraging results.)
sentenceTranslation: The first quarter was disappointing; on the other hand, the second quarter shows very encouraging results.
grammarPointLabel: en revanche — opposition de résultats contrastés (registre professionnel)
explanation: en revanche is the standard formal connector for business or report contexts where two contrasting results are presented. par contre would be inappropriate in this register.

quizQuestion:
Il est parti sans laisser d'adresse ; {___}, il ne veut plus avoir de contact avec nous.
(He left without leaving an address; in other words, he no longer wants contact with us.)
sentenceTranslation: He left without leaving an address; in other words, he no longer wants contact with us.
explanation: autrement dit (in other words) introduces a simpler or more direct restatement of the preceding idea. Slightly more concise than en d'autres termes.

quizQuestion:
Il est bilingue, {___} qu'il parle deux langues couramment.
(He is bilingual, meaning he speaks two languages fluently.)
sentenceTranslation: He is bilingual, meaning he speaks two languages fluently.
explanation: c'est-à-dire (that is / meaning) follows a term being defined with an explanatory clause. When followed by que + clause, both c'est-à-dire que and c'est-à-dire alone are correct.

quizQuestion:
Le budget a été intégralement dépensé ; {___}, il ne reste plus rien pour les imprévus.
(The budget has been completely spent; in other words, nothing remains for unexpected costs.)
sentenceTranslation: The budget has been completely spent; in other words, nothing remains for unexpected costs.
explanation: en d'autres termes (in other terms / in other words) is slightly more formal than autrement dit. Both restate the preceding idea in a clearer or more explicit way.

quizQuestion:
Le rapport est incomplet, les données sont erronées et le délai est dépassé. {___}, ce projet est un échec total.
(The report is incomplete, data are wrong, and the deadline is missed. In sum, this project is a complete failure.)
sentenceTranslation: In sum, this project is a complete failure.
explanation: en somme (in sum / all in all) draws a conclusion from a series of facts. Unlike autrement dit, it summarises multiple points rather than reformulating one.

quizQuestion:
Le contrat dure deux ans, {___} vingt-quatre mois.
(The contract lasts two years, that is, twenty-four months.)
sentenceTranslation: The contract lasts two years, that is, twenty-four months.
explanation: soit used as a connector (not the subjunctive of être) means 'that is' or 'namely' in formal and legal writing. It introduces an equivalent numerical or definitional reformulation.

quizQuestion:
Partez maintenant ; {___}, vous raterez votre train.
(Leave now; otherwise, you will miss your train.)
sentenceTranslation: Leave now; otherwise, you will miss your train.
explanation: autrement in this usage means 'otherwise' or 'or else'. It signals that failing to perform the previously mentioned action will lead to a negative consequence. Distinct from autrement dit.

quizQuestion:
Les négociations n'ont abouti à aucun accord. {___}, tout est à refaire.
(The negotiations reached no agreement. In plain terms, everything must start over.)
sentenceTranslation: In plain terms, everything must start over.
grammarPointLabel: en clair — reformulation directe et tranchante
explanation: en clair (in plain language / in clear terms) reformulates a complex or diplomatic statement in stark, direct terms. Often used journalistically to cut through official jargon.

quizQuestion:
Il n'a {___} rien mangé depuis deux jours.
(He has eaten next to nothing, so to speak, for two days.)
sentenceTranslation: He has eaten next to nothing, so to speak, for two days.
grammarPointLabel: pour ainsi dire — atténuation / reformulation nuancée
explanation: pour ainsi dire (so to speak / as it were) softens a statement, signalling that the speaker uses an approximate expression. Used when the literal wording might overstate the case.

quizQuestion:
Ce produit est moins cher que les autres ; {___}, il est de meilleure qualité.
(This product is cheaper than the others; furthermore, it is of better quality.)
sentenceTranslation: This product is cheaper than the others; furthermore, it is of better quality.
explanation: de plus (furthermore / moreover) adds a supporting argument to strengthen the previous point. Neutral register; used in both spoken and written French.

quizQuestion:
Ce médicament est efficace ; {___}, il présente peu d'effets secondaires.
(This medication is effective; furthermore, it has few side effects.)
sentenceTranslation: This medication is effective; furthermore, it has few side effects.
explanation: en outre (furthermore / in addition) is a formal connector adding a distinct argument. More literary than de plus; preferred in academic writing and official reports.

quizQuestion:
La ville offre de nombreuses opportunités professionnelles. {___}, son réseau de transports en commun est très développé.
(The city offers many professional opportunities. Besides, its public transport network is very well developed.)
sentenceTranslation: The city offers many professional opportunities. Besides, its public transport network is very well developed.
grammarPointLabel: par ailleurs — addition d'un point distinct
explanation: par ailleurs (besides / on another note) introduces a separate, additional point from a different angle. It does not simply reinforce the previous point but adds an independent consideration.

quizQuestion:
Cette décision est injuste, et {___}, elle est illégale.
(This decision is unfair, and what is more, it is illegal.)
sentenceTranslation: This decision is unfair, and what is more, it is illegal.
grammarPointLabel: qui plus est — addition escalatoire (registre formel)
explanation: qui plus est (what is more / furthermore) adds a point that surpasses and reinforces the preceding one, often increasing severity or importance. Formal register; used in written argumentation.

quizQuestion:
Il est non seulement compétent, {___} il est passionné par son travail.
(He is not only competent, but also passionate about his work.)
sentenceTranslation: He is not only competent, but also passionate about his work.
explanation: non seulement…mais aussi (not only…but also) is a paired structure adding a second, equally important or superior quality. mais encore is an equivalent, slightly more formal alternative.

quizQuestion:
Il a travaillé sans relâche toute la semaine et, {___}, il a rendu le rapport en avance.
(He worked relentlessly all week and, on top of that, submitted the report early.)
sentenceTranslation: He worked relentlessly all week and, on top of that, submitted the report early.
explanation: de surcroît (on top of that / to boot) adds a further point that goes beyond what was already impressive. Very formal; found in legal, administrative, and literary texts.

quizQuestion:
Ce résultat est surprenant, {___} choquant pour certains observateurs.
(This result is surprising, even shocking for some observers.)
sentenceTranslation: This result is surprising, even shocking for some observers.
explanation: voire (even / or even) adds a stronger or more extreme version of the preceding adjective or statement, implying escalation: X, or indeed Y. Formal register; not to be confused with voir (to see).

quizQuestion:
Le projet respecte les délais impartis. {___}, il se situe en dessous du budget prévu.
(The project meets the deadlines. Furthermore, it comes in under the planned budget.)
sentenceTranslation: The project meets the deadlines. Furthermore, it comes in under the planned budget.
grammarPointLabel: en outre — addition d'argument positif supplémentaire
explanation: en outre adds a separate advantage (being under budget) to the first (meeting deadlines). par ailleurs would also work but signals a more thematically distinct additional point.

quizQuestion:
Ce candidat est expérimenté et, {___}, il connaît parfaitement le secteur.
(This candidate is experienced and, what is more, knows the sector perfectly.)
sentenceTranslation: This candidate is experienced and, what is more, knows the sector perfectly.
explanation: qui plus est (what is more / and furthermore) adds a culminating argument that tops the previous one. Used to intensify the cumulative effect of listed qualities.
